revision.text:
[[ja:叡福寺北古墳]] {{Infobox tumulus | name = Eifuku-ji North Tumulus | image = [[File:Eifukuji Kita Kofun, enkei-3.jpg|280px]]
Distant view of the burial mound]] | alias = Prince Shōtoku's Grave / Kamishiro Tumulus {{Sfn|Prince Shōtoku's Grave (Heibonsha)|1986|qq=}} | group = {{ill|Isonokami Valley Tumuli|ja|磯長谷古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Valley Tumuli|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588120}} | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10856365}}, Taishi
(Within {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|qid=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji|1=Eifuku-ji}}) | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 31 | latitude_sec = 11.23 | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 38 | longitude_sec = 21.38 | ISO = JP-27 | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|lt=Round tumulus|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | size = 43m north-south, 53m east-west
Height: 11m | era = Late 7th century | burial = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (with three coffins inside) | interred = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designation): {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}
(Archaeological hypothesis): Prince Shōtoku (Umayado no Ōji), {{ill|Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|en|Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|qid=Q11597250|lt=Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|1=Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}}, and {{ill|Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume|ja|膳部菩岐々美郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume|1=Kashiwade no Oiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612414}} | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Dry-lacquer coffin|lt=Dry-lacquer coffin|lt=Dry lacquer coffin|ja|乾漆棺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffin|qq=}} and more | mausoleum = Imperial Household Agency designation: "Isonaga no Misasagi" }} [[File:Eifukuji Kita Kofun, haisho-3.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|{{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku's Tomb|1=Prince Shōtoku}} at Isonaga Mausoleum, place of worship|qq=}}]] The '''Eifuku-ji North Tumulus''' (叡福寺北古墳) is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|lt=Round tumulus|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|1=Taishichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10856365}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli forming part of the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Tumuli|ja|磯長谷古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Valley Tumuli|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588120}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "Isonaga no Misasagi," the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku (Umayado no Ōji), the Crown Prince of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}. Archaeological studies also strongly suggest that it is Prince Shōtoku's burial site. == Overview == [[File:Ekifukuji (Taishi) kondou and Eifukuji Kita Kofun-2.jpg|thumb|200px|{{center|{{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|qid=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji|1=Eifuku-ji}} Main Hall and Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|qq=}}]] The tumulus is located on the southern slope of a hill at the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, in the Isonokami Valley area. It faces south and is part of a broader group of tumuli, including four imperial mausolea, collectively recognized as the {{ill|Isonokami Valley Tumuli|ja|磯長谷古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Valley Tumuli|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588120}}. The tumulus is situated within the northern grounds of {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|qid=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji|1=Eifuku-ji}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, which has conducted surveys. Additionally, drawings and descriptions from before the Meiji period offer valuable historical records. The tumulus has an elliptical shape, measuring approximately 43 meters north-south and 53 meters east-west, with a height of 11 meters. It consists of three construction tiers. The surface lacks stone paving or decorative stones. Its burial chamber is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. Within the chamber, three burial platforms for coffins remain, and fragments of a {{ill|Dry-lacquer coffin|lt=Dry-lacquer coffin|lt=Dry lacquer coffin|ja|乾漆棺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffin|qq=}} have been discovered. Over time, modifications have been made, including the addition of two concentric rows of "boundary stones" (''kekkai-seki'') around the base and the construction of a protective roofed structure at the entrance. The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 7th century, during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. While the Imperial Household Agency designates it as the tomb of Prince Shōtoku (died 622 CE), the site is also considered a joint burial mound for Prince Shōtoku, his mother {{ill|Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|en|Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|qid=Q11597250|lt=Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|1=Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}} (Emperor Yōmei's consort), and his consort {{ill|Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume|ja|膳部菩岐々美郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume|1=Kashiwade no Oiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612414}}. == Current Access == '''Location:''' * {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|qid=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji|1=Eifuku-ji}}, Taishi, Osaka Prefecture '''Transportation:''' * From {{ill|Kishi Station (Osaka)|en|Kishi Station (Osaka)|qid=Q6416576|lt=Kishi Station|1=Kishi Station (Osaka)}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} Nagano Line): Take the Kishi Circulation Bus (Routes 62 or 63). * From {{ill|Kaminotaishi Station|en|Kaminotaishi Station|qid=Q6358219|lt=Shōdenji Station|1=Kaminotaishi Station}} (Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line): Take the "Taishi Nottoko Bus." * Alight at "Shōtoku Taishi Goryōmae" bus stop. The tumulus is a 3-minute walk from the stop. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|qid=Q4557237|lt=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|1=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}: Features full-scale reconstructions and models related to the tumulus. * Taishi Town Historical Road Museum: Exhibits models and artifacts related to Eifuku-ji North Tumulus. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} * {{ill|Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|en|Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|qid=Q11597250|lt=Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko|1=Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}} * {{ill|Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume|ja|膳部菩岐々美郎女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade no Hokikimi no Iratsume|1=Kashiwade no Oiratsume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612414}} * {{ill|Eifuku-ji|en|Eifuku-ji|qid=Q11410732|lt=Eifuku-ji|1=Eifuku-ji}} * {{ill|Isonokami Valley Tumuli|ja|磯長谷古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Valley Tumuli|1=Shinagadani Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588120}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|叡福寺北古墳|version=104346646|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:叡福寺北古墳]] {{Commons category|Eifukuji Kita Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q48743902}} [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Prince Shōtoku]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
